free_text: Values Superseded
(p,n) Cross Sections of V51, Cr52, Cu63, Cu65, Ag107,
Ag109, Cd111, Cd114, and La139 from 5 to 10.5 MeV
Many of the targets included an additional Cu foil
in order to compare directly between the Cu65(p,n)
cross sections and those of the other target foils.
In a few runs V, Cr, Cu, and Ag foils were irradiated
together in order to cross check the results.
Incident proton energy of 10.5 MeV was degraded by
metallic absorbers of known thicknesses placed in
front of the target foils. The energy degradation was
calculated according to R.M.Sternheimer, Phys.Rev.
The loss of reaction products by recoil was very small
and therefore neglected.
Corrections were made for the escape-peak probability,
self-absorption in the target foil and absorption in
the detector window.
Data errors given include uncertainties in
. beam current measurements,
. target weights,
. counting efficiencies,
. counting statistics,
. conversion coefficients,
. decay branching ratios,
. half-life values.
The total errors in the cross sections are thought to
be 10% or less for most of the targets.
Uncertainties in the proton energy; about
0.5 MeV at 5 MeV and increasing tP about 0.15 MeV at
10 MeV.
The experimental cross sections were compared
with some total proton reaction cross sections
obtained from Optical-Model caculations.
Data taken from Table II in main reference.
Converted from data set B0065;
updated at NNDC. Originally compiled by KaChaPaG and
updated by CaJaD.
OS. Important correction in sub.3, several
BIB updates

equal to Cu.

Counting efficiency determined using 22Na standard.
According to Colle et al. the cross sections have to
be multiplied with a factor of 1.002 due to a change
in the beta+ abundance from 0.93 to 0.928.
Natural Cu foils, thickness approximately 5 mg/cm**2.
According to Colle et al. the cross sections have to
be multiplied with a factor of 0.968 due to a change
in the gamma ray abundance from 0.49 to 0.506.
Natural Ag foils, thickness approximately 5 mg/cm**2.
Abundance calculated by the KaChaPaG compiler using
data given in the publication.
According to Colle et al., the cross sections should
be multiplied with a factor of 1.035 due to a change
in the gamma ray abundance from 0.049 to 0.0476.
** COMMENT BY COMPILER **  This factor seems to be
incorrect, since due to the decay data given, the
gamma ray abundance used was 0.0469 and therefore,
the resulting correction factor is 0.985.

Assuming the range-energy relationship for Cd to be
equal to Ag.

Assuming the range-energy relationship for Cd to be

Metallic La volatized onto 12.7 micrometer thick
Al foils.
Bi evaporated onto 12.7 micrometer thick Al foils.
209Bi data were measured to check previously
published values of C.G.Andre et al. Experimental
details and further cross sections between 6 and
10.6 MeV are given in this reference.
